🕷️ Add realistic scraping implementation guide

Reality check: Not all platforms are equally scrapeable!

✅ EASY TO SCRAPE (80-95% success):
- Reddit (old.reddit.com) - 90-95%
- Amazon Q&A (same page as reviews) - 85-90%

⚠️ MEDIUM DIFFICULTY (60-80%):
- YouTube Comments (JavaScript required) - 60-70%

❌ TOO HARD (30-50%):
- Forums (varied structures, login required)
- Twitter (heavily protected, ToS violation)

REVISED PLAN:
Focus on The Big 3 (Reddit + Amazon Q&A + YouTube)
- Coverage: 85-90%

📋 Feature 2 coverage strategy - Multi-source 'Community Truth Bomb'

Problem: Reddit only covers 40-60% of products
Solution: Smart fallback chain with 5 sources

Priority order:
1. Reddit (best quality, 40-60% coverage)
2. Amazon Q&A (best coverage, 80%+)
3. YouTube comments (50-70%, long-term reviews)
4. Forums (30-40%, niche products)
5. Twitter (50%+, real-time complaints)

Combined coverage: 95%+ of products!

Benefits:
- Better coverage (95% vs 40-60%)
- More reliable (cross-validation)

feat: stamp every event with hostname and optional instanceId

Adds `hostname` (OS machine name, captured once at startup via node:os)
and `instanceId` (user-configured label) to TelemetryEventBase so every
log line identifies which OpenClaw install produced it — essential when
logs from multiple machines or deployments flow into the same JSONL file,
syslog stream, or GCP API Gateway endpoint.

- src/types.ts: add `hostname` and `instanceId` to TelemetryEventBase;
  omit both from TelemetryEvent

feat: add `openclaw omo-init` CLI command for handbook bootstrapping

Register a CLI subcommand via api.registerCli() so users can explicitly
initialize handbook directories and scripts after plugin install, without

uses the project's own build script instead of npx resolving to
the wrong tsc package.

fix: create ~/.openclaw before npm install in Dockerfile

Omniclaw's postinstall script runs `ln -sfn "$PWD" ~/.openclaw/current-plugin`
which fails during docker build because /home/node/.openclaw/ doesn't exist yet
(it's only created at runtime via volume mount). Pre-creating the directory
lets the symlink succeed during build.

[#2226] Fix DER INTEGER encoding for Node 25 strict OpenSSL (#2227)

Strip leading zero bytes in derInteger() before encoding. randomBytes(16)
can produce values starting with 0x00, which is illegal padding in strict
DER encoding enforced by Node 25's OpenSSL.

Closes #2226

✨ feat(agent): add per-chat message queue and /abort command

Per-chat SemaphoreSlim ensures only one AI response at a time.
Messages arriving during a response are persisted with queued=true
(invisible to AI context), flushed after response with updated
timestamps. /abort cancels active AI response via CancellationToken.
Queued messages get clock emoji reaction, removed on flush.
Crash recovery flushes leftover queued messages on startup.

Fix node host pairing: use openclaw devices approve, pass --host/--port

The node host was installing but never connecting because:
1. Pairing approval uses `openclaw devices approve <id>`, not `nodes approve`
2. Node host needs explicit --host and --port to find the gateway
3. Pending pairing requests expire after 5 minutes — must approve quickly

Changes:
- Pass --host 127.0.0.1 --port <gw_port> to node install/run
- Use --force flag to overwrite existing node installation
- Auto-approve via `

fix: remove version cache and require metadata verification to prevent infinite upgrade loop

The previous fix (b17fa17) read from output-metadata.json instead of
package.json, but cached the result forever.  If the first read failed
(file locked, missing, or not yet flushed), the package.json fallback
was permanently cached — reporting a version ahead of the actual APK.

Two changes:
1. Remove _pkgCache: always re-read from disk (cheap for 30-min checks)
2. Only set apkAvailable=true when versi
env vars for trustedProxies and port

Critical fix: install node host — the missing piece for agent tools

Root cause found: the agent had ZERO tools because no node host was
connected (openclaw nodes status → Known: 0, Paired: 0, Connected: 0).

In OpenClaw architecture:
- Gateway = handles messages, routes to agents
- Node host = provides tools (browser, exec, camera, screen)
Without a running node host, the agent only has messaging — no browser,
no exec, no file tools.

Changes:
- install.sh: Added node host install + start + wai

fix: tick timer auto-cycles and bootstraps from welcome message

The tick timer previously clamped at 100% and stopped when no new
action_result messages arrived (e.g. after an error or when idle).
Now it auto-cycles using modulo, estimates tick numbers from elapsed
time, bootstraps from the welcome message's current_tick/server_time,
and resets baseline when get_status polling detects drift.

fix: isolate e2e tests from user config and stop persisting ephemeral messages

E2e tests (api-auth, database-api) were writing to the real ~/.milady/milady.json,
corrupting user config on every test run. Both now use MILADY_CONFIG_PATH pointed
at a temp directory.

via WebSocket but no longer persisted to the database. These accounted for ~83% of
all stored messages with no long-term value.

fix: web build stuck loading due to @tauri-apps module resolution

1. localFetch.ts was statically imported in main.tsx, causing Rollup
   to keep the externalized bare specifier "import(@tauri-apps/plugin-http)"
   in the web bundle. Browsers cannot resolve bare module specifiers,
   causing a fatal error on page load.

   Fix: use __BUILD_TARGET__ compile-time guard with dynamic import so
   Rollup eliminates the entire localFetch import as dead code in
   web/capacitor builds.

Fix agent setup: update main agent identity instead of creating new agent

The main agent handles all Telegram/Discord messages by default. Creating
a new agent via `agents add` left it isolated with no message routing.

agent via `agents set-identity --agent main`, so the content engine
capabilities apply to all incoming messages immediately.

feat(proxy,config): integrate context engine, threat intel, semantic cache

Proxy pipeline (17 phases):
- _phase_threat_intel: after policy, before model_router. 403 on
  critical threats, X-Orchesis-Threat-* headers for warnings.
- _phase_context: after secrets, before upstream. Optimizes messages,
  X-Orchesis-Context-Tokens-Saved header.
- Semantic cache: lookup in _phase_upstream before HTTP call, store
  in _phase_post_upstream after 200. X-Orchesis-Cache headers.

_RequestContext: context_

feat: Semantic Cache  fuzzy response caching without external deps

Add semantic_cache.py using SimHash (Charikar 2002) + trigram Jaccard.
No numpy, no vector DB  pure stdlib.

Two-stage lookup: exact hash O(1)  SimHash candidate scan  Jaccard
verification. Structural key (model+roles+tools) prevents cross-
contamination. Conservative defaults: Hamming 8, Jaccard 0.6.

LRU eviction with TTL, model filter, tool_call exclusion,
token/cost savings tracking. Thread-safe.

Tests: 50 passed.

indefinitely in some environments, causing all pairing tests to
hang. Reverted to fastrand which is sufficient: pairing codes are
8 chars with 15-min TTL and 10-attempt brute-force lockout, making
CSPRNG quality irrelevant to security.

[#2186] Fix gateway_connection migration test: roll back correct number of steps (#2222)

The down-migration test hardcoded 'rollback 1 step' assuming migration 135
was the latest. With new migrations 136-139 added in Phase 0, rolling back
1 step only removed migration 139, leaving the gateway_connection table
intact. Now dynamically calculates the number of migrations to roll back.

Co-authored-by: security-agent-1 <security-agent-1@devcontainer-security.local>

fix(bridge): bound SendAndReceive with a 10-minute per-message timeout

A stalled gateway (ZeroClaw hanging mid-response) would block the
handler goroutine indefinitely. Wrapping SendAndReceive with a derived
context ensures the goroutine always unblocks, the typing indicator
stops, and the connection is evicted for reconnect on the next message.

10 minutes matches the OpenClaw gateway's own response timeout.

but kwargs is not in the method signature. This causes a NameError when
the Telegram channel is instantiated, making it completely unusable.

Also fixes the type hint from undefined TelegramConfig to the correct
TelegramChannelConfig which is already imported.

Full OpenClaw restart: kill all processes, start fresh, verify browser

Step 6/7 now performs a complete restart:
1. Graceful gateway stop
2. Kill ALL openclaw processes by port (lsof/fuser on macOS/Linux,
   netstat+taskkill+wmic on Windows) and by name (pkill -f)
3. Start gateway with --force flag to claim port
4. Poll RPC probe for up to 30s until gateway responds
5. Verify browser tool is active after restart

Handles macOS (lsof + pkill), Linux (fuser + pkill), Windows (netstat
+ taskkill +

yourself' instead of using its exec tool autonomously. Root cause: the
instructions were ambiguous — showing code blocks without explicitly stating
the assistant must invoke exec as a tool call, not relay commands to the user.

Changes:
- Add critical banner at top: assistant has terminal access via exec tool,
  must NEVER ask the user to run commands
- Rewrite cr

Auto-restart gateway after install to load new config immediately

The installer now kills any running gateway process, starts it fresh
with the new config (lobster, llm-task, sandbox off, tools allow all),

versions handle this across all OS (netstat/lsof/fuser for port kill).

fix(bundle): use CJS format so jiti loads plugin synchronously

jiti loads ESM via native import() which returns a Promise.
OpenClaw detects the Promise return from register() and drops it with
'async registration is ignored', silently skipping all tool/service registrations.
CJS is loaded synchronously by jiti, matching what OpenClaw expects.

status check in branch protection.

fix(checklist): source GOG_KEYRING_PASSWORD from .env on headless Linux

gog auth list --json fails silently on headless Linux when
GOG_KEYRING_PASSWORD isn't set (no TTY for the keyring prompt).
The password is typically in ~/.openclaw/.env but only loaded by
the gateway process. Now the Google check sources it automatically,
so gog auth list --json works in the full mode instead of falling
through to the degraded keyring-filename fallback.

refactor: Extract JWT secret retrieval into a separate function

- Created a `getJwtSecret` function to encapsulate the logic for retrieving the JWT secret, improving code readability and maintainability.
- Updated token signing and verification functions to use the new `getJwtSecret` function instead of directly accessing the JWT secret constant.

feat: migrate to Changesets + main-only branching strategy (#32)

## Summary

Migrates DeClaw's release process from Git Flow + manual release scripts
to **Changesets + main-only** branching — aligned with mastra,
langchain, and OpenClaw itself.

## What changed

### Workflow (developer-facing)
- **Before**: `PR → develop → bash scripts/release.sh → release/* PR →
main → backmerge`
- **After**: `PR → main (with .changeset/*.md) → Changesets auto-creates
Version PR → merge → auto-publish`

fix(checklist): discover gog accounts from Linux keyring dir

On Linux, gog stores tokens as files named 'token:<email>' in
~/.config/gogcli/keyring/. The Google check only looked at macOS
Keychain, so Linux deployments (like Spock) always fell through
to the 'Could not determine primary account' warning.

Now scans the keyring directory filenames on Linux to extract
authenticated email addresses.

fix(slack): record app_mention retry key before dedupe check (#37033)

- Prime app_mention retry allowance before dedupe so near-simultaneous message/app_mention races do not drop valid mentions.
- Prevent duplicate dispatch when app_mention wins the race and message prepare later succeeds.
- Prune dispatched mention keys and add regression coverage for both dropped and successful in-flight message outcomes.

🐛[FIX] Cast chunk to any to bypass TS type narrowing for tool-input chunks

TypeScript's type narrowing on the discriminated union may prevent
matching 'tool-input-start'/'tool-input-delta' at runtime. Cast chunk
to any and also handle both possible ID fields (toolCallId vs id)
and delta fields (inputTextDelta vs delta) for AI SDK v6 compatibility.

Add stall detection to kill hung processes early

Claude processes were sitting idle for 60+ minutes waiting on tool
calls that never returned, until the overall timeout fired. Add a
stall detector (default 5 min, configurable via CLAUDE_STALL_TIMEOUT_MS)
that kills the process if no stdout output is received within the
window. Applies to both callClaude and the scheduler's runClaude.

Also adds SIGKILL fallback to callClaude (scheduler already had it).

🐛[FIX] Stream tool_call in real-time, show tool_result only on step finish

Root cause: onChunk is a synchronous callback — React 18 batches all
state updates from tool-call, tool-call-delta, tool-result into a single
render, so the user sees everything appear at once.

Fix:
- onChunk: only handle tool-call-delta (stream raw args text) and
  text-delta. Record tool-call name but don't finalize or create result.
- onStepFinish: finalize tool_call (formatted JSON, streaming=false)
  and create to

fix: replace crypto.randomUUID() with fallback uuid utility for browser compatibility

In non-secure contexts (HTTP) or older browsers/WebViews, crypto.randomUUID()
is not available, causing WebSocket handshake to fail silently and the dashboard
to stay stuck on "loading" forever.

- Add src/lib/uuid.ts with getRandomValues()-based fallback
- Replace all 3 crypto.randomUUID() call sites (ws-client, rpc-client, ws-adapter)

fix: bypass auth for browser navigation requests so SPA routes load remotely

When accessing the app via tunnel (e.g., mac.enso.net/chat), client-side
routes don't match static files and fall through to the auth middleware,
which blocks them with 401 Unauthorized. Browser navigation requests
(sec-fetch-mode: navigate) now bypass auth since they only need the HTML
shell — actual data is fetched via authenticated API calls afterward.

fix: use --reset and juju-wait for context-window disable test steps

Setting ai-context-window=0 may not trigger config-changed when 0 is
the config default. Use 'juju config --reset' which always fires the
hook regardless of the current value. Replace 'sleep 30' with
'juju-wait -v -t 120' for more reliable synchronization.

Also improve test step names to be more descriptive (include actual
values and expected outcomes).
Full GCE & Text/Whatsapp integration

fix: add brew shellenv to ~/.bash_profile for non-interactive login shells

Ubuntu's ~/.bashrc exits immediately for non-interactive shells due to
'case $- in *i*) ;; *) return;; esac'. When CI runs
'sudo -u ubuntu bash -l -c "brew ..."' (login but non-interactive),
bash reads ~/.bash_profile but never sources ~/.bashrc, leaving brew
PATH unset and causing 'brew: command not found'.

Also detect brew prefix dynamically (checks linuxbrew and user-local
locations) instead of hardcoding /home/linux

Connect dashboard chat to actual OpenClaw agent via WebSocket ACP

Dashboard chat now connects to the gateway via its native WebSocket
protocol (ACP - Agent Control Protocol) instead of the non-existent
REST endpoint. The /api/chat route:

1. Opens a WebSocket to the gateway
2. Handles connect.challenge → connect handshake with gateway token
3. Sends chat.send with the user's message
4. Streams the response via chat events (delta/final)
5. Falls back to direct Anthropic call if gateway is unreac

verifiers, CSRF state parameters, and webhook secrets. thread_rng() uses a
userspace CSPRNG (ChaCha) seeded from OS entropy, which is fine for
non-security contexts but adds an unnecessary

fix: prevent concurrent memory hygiene passes and Windows file lock errors (#535)

* fix: prevent concurrent memory hygiene passes and Windows file lock errors (#495)

The heartbeat system spawns hygiene passes via tokio::spawn on every
tick, creating a TOCTOU race where multiple tasks read the state file
before any saves, causing all to execute concurrently. On Windows this
also triggers OS error 1224 (file locked by memory-mapped section)
when multiple tasks call std::fs::write on the same fil

feat(cli): bulletproof init+start flow for first-time users (#678)

Three changes to make onboarding reliable:

1. Auto-init on start: 'reflectt start' auto-creates ~/.reflectt/
   if missing (no separate 'reflectt init' required)

2. Health check after detached start: 'reflectt start -d' polls
   /health for up to 10s and confirms server is responding before
   printing success message

3. Auto-fix port drift: 'reflectt status' now auto-repairs
   config.json port when it detects the server on

fix(scanner): resolve lan_host from registry for multi-NIC dedup

When ActiveScanner probes via different IPs (LAN vs WireGuard), it used
the raw IP as lan_host, preventing multi-NIC deduplication. Now checks
if the same agent_id+port already exists in the registry with a real
hostname (from mDNS/LocalProbe) and reuses it, enabling correct merge.

Adds 3 test cases for the new resolveHostForDedup logic (365 total).
